data.title: Authum Investment Declares Interim Dividends for Financial Year 2024-25
data.description:
Authum Investment & Infrastructure declared an interim dividend of 7% of face value 10 Indian rupees each fully paid up preference shares for the financial year 2024-25, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company also declared an interim dividend of 100% of face value 1 rupee each fully paid up equity shares for the financial year 2024-25.

The record date for to decide on the eligible shareholders for the dividend has been fixed as March 27 and the dividend is to be paid by April 19.

Shares of the company were down over 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Eureka Group Holdings Appoints Michael Copeland as Acting Chief Financial Officer
data.description:
Eureka Group Holdings appointed Michael Copeland as acting Chief Financial Officer effective March 22, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

Copeland is currently the group financial controller at the company and is a senior finance leader with over 20 years of experience primarily across the financial and professional services industries in both Australia and the UK, the filing said.

An executive search to identify a permanent CFO is underway, the filing added.

The company's shares rose 2% in recent Friday trade.

data.title: India's VA Tech Wabag gains on $100 mln equity partnership
data.description:
** VA Tech Wabag jumps 7.8% to 1,491.9 rupees

** Water treatment firm enters into non-binding equity partnership of $100 mln with an investor consortium, including Norfund, for dedicated platform

** Municipal platform to focus on development of capital projects for the municipal sector

** More than 853,000 shares traded, 1.4x its 30-day avg

** Stock up 6% so far this month, on track to snap three month losing streak

data.title: Nikkei Falls 0.2%, Dragged by Heavy-Industry Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: The Nikkei Stock Average closed 0.2% lower at 37677.06 as falls in heavy-industry shares offset gains in bank stocks. IHI Corp. fell 4.9% and Kawasaki Heavy Industries dropped 3.2%. Meanwhile, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group gained 5.7% and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group added 3.7% after stronger-than-expected domestic inflation data raised expectations that the Bank of Japan would continue to raise rates gradually. Broader benchmark Topix rose 0.3% to 2804.16. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield gained half a basis point to 1.520%. U.S. trade and foreign policies are in focus. USD/JPY is at 149.50, compared with 148.78 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Higher German Borrowing in Coming Years Likely to Prop Up Yields — Market Talk
data.description: An anticipated rise in German borrowing in the coming years suggests higher government bond yields, Mediolanum International Funds' Niall Scanlon says. "All else being equal, additional supply leads to higher bond yields," the fixed income portfolio manager says. Mediolanum thinks the market has already priced in a lot of the German fiscal announcement and associated additional supply. The rise in German 10-year Bund yields in recent weeks reflects expectations of a higher European Central Bank policy rate on a stronger growth outlook arising from more expansionary German fiscal policy, he says. However, it also reflects an increase in term premium--the compensation investors demand for holding longer-dated bonds, Scanlon says. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Forex and Fixed Income Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering FX and Fixed Income. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0654 GMT - The Nikkei Stock Average closed 0.2% lower at 37677.06 as falls in heavy-industry shares offset gains in bank stocks. IHI Corp. fell 4.9% and Kawasaki Heavy Industries dropped 3.2%. Meanwhile, Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group gained 5.7% and Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group added 3.7% after stronger-than-expected domestic inflation data raised expectations that the Bank of Japan would continue to raise rates gradually. Broader benchmark Topix rose 0.3% to 2804.16. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield gained half a basis point to 1.520%. U.S. trade and foreign policies are in focus. USD/JPY is at 149.50, compared with 148.78 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0652 GMT - Germany is expected to leave government bond issuance in 2025 broadly in line with the previous guidance, Mediolanum International Funds' Niall Scanlon says. "However, we expect extra supply of German government bonds over the coming years, beginning from 2026 as the country moves to fund the proposed fiscal expansion," the fixed income portfolio manager says. The German Finance Agency will publish the review of its borrowing plans for 2Q on Monday. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

0640 GMT - Eurozone government bond yield spreads over German Bunds continue to trade in a tight range despite the recent increase in rates volatility, say Societe Generale Research's rates strategists in a note. The spreads could even narrow further, albeit at a slow and gradual manner, to be driven by the anticipated deterioration in Germany's fiscal metrics, they say. However, "if the market focuses on the impact of more spending on public finances, then Germany would be seen as remaining in better shape than others," they say. Tariff negotiations, fears over the eurozone's growth outlook and European Central Bank policy decisions are playing in the background, they say. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Newborn Town's 2024 Profit Slides 6%; Shares Fall 12%
data.description:
Newborn Town's attributable profit fell 6.3% to 480.3 million yuan in 2024 from 512.8 million yuan in 2023, according to a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Earnings per share at the social networking company decreased to 0.42 yuan from 0.45 yuan in the previous year.

Revenue grew 54% to 5.09 billion yuan from 3.31 billion yuan a year earlier.

Shares plunged 12% during Friday's afternoon trading.

data.title: Hang Lung Group Posts Lower 2024 Profit
data.description:
Hang Lung Group reported a profit attributable to the shareholders for 2024 of HK$1.61 billion, down from HK$2.81 billion in the previous year, according to a filing with the Hong Kong bourse on Thursday.

Earnings per share were HK$1.18, down from HK$2.06 a year ago.

Revenue for the year was HK$11.76 billion from HK$10.88 billion in the prior year.

The board recommended a final dividend of HK$0.65 per share, payable on June 16.

The company's shares were down 2% in recent trading.

data.title: France's Elior Group to set up Asia's first aircraft parts hub in Hong Kong
data.description:
French aeronautic services company Elior Group will set up its Asia headquarters for aircraft parts dismantling, recycling and trading in Hong Kong, which could elevate the city's status as a regional aviation hub.

Elior, a subsidiary of the Derichebourg Group, has signed a memorandum of understanding on the move with the Airport Authority Hong Kong (AAHK), the Hong Kong government said in a press release on Friday.

Hong Kong's status as an Asian aviation hub has been hit by a drop in passenger and cargo flights in recent years due to disruptions from COVID-19 and a decrease in flights using Russia's airspace due to the war in Ukraine.

The agreement with Elior Group is the government's latest attempt to help boost the sector, which is starting to restore flights back to pre-pandemic levels and inaugurate new routes to mainland China and beyond.

Secretary for Transport and Logistics Mable Chan said Elior and AAHK's partnership would enrich Hong Kong's position as an international aviation hub, and support "the aviation development of our country as well as the Asian region as a whole."

Hong Kong will also become the first aircraft parts processing and trading centre in Asia through this deal.

There is growing demand globally for recycled aircraft parts - from fuselages and engines to doors and flight controls - at cheaper prices as commercial fleets age.

The Asia-Pacific region has emerged as an important market for such services due to its growing aircraft fleet driven by low-cost and regional travel.

"We look forward to further developing and exploring new opportunities in Hong Kong, and from here expand across Asia," Elior Chairman and CEO Daniel Derichebourg said.

data.title: Singapore's Inflation Likely Eased in February, WSJ Poll Shows — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore CPI likely rose 0.9% on year in February, after January's 1.2% increase, according to the median estimate of eight economists polled by The Wall Street Journal. Core inflation is projected to have cooled to 0.7% last month from January's 0.8% increase, according to seven of the economists surveyed. Headline inflation likely eased due to lower import costs and broad-based moderation across major goods and services categories, say ING analysts and economists in a note. A significant fall in electricity prices and a high base comparison on year are also expected to drive a cooling inflation reading, says Moody's Analytics associate economist Eugene Tan in an email. The data are due Monday.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Poland - Factors to Watch March 21
data.description:
The following are significant news stories, press reports and events on Friday. ALL TIMES GMT (Poland: GMT + 1 hour):

WAGE PRESSURE

Wage growth and the wage-price spiral in Poland are crucial in the fight against inflation. The partial data presented by the statistical office are better (for disinflation) than expected and confirm the phasing out of inflation processes. This "bodes very well for the future," Monetary Policy Council member Ireneusz Dabrowski wrote on X social media platform.

LOAN SETTLEMENTS

At the end of 2024, eight Polish banks listed on the Warsaw Stock Exchange concluded 123,000 settlements with holders of mortgage loans in Swiss francs, which is 42% more than at the end of 2023, Rzeczpospolita daily reported, citing its own calculations.

EBRD ON POLAND

The chief economist of the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD), Beata Javorcik, said that Poland looks very good in the region - the Polish economy is larger and more diversified, it can count on domestic demand and is less dependent on export markets, Parkiet daily reported.

ALIOR BANK

The strategic goal of Alior Bank is to regularly pay out dividends and grow faster than the market average in all business segments, chief executive Piotr Zabski told Puls Biznesu daily.

****Reuters has not verified stories reported by Polish media and does not vouch for their accuracy.****

data.title: 17Live Group Buys Back More Shares
data.description:
17Live Group bought back 30,000 shares in the open market on Thursday for SG$27,590, according to a same-day filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The streaming services company has been authorized to repurchase around 17.7 million shares under its current mandate. To date, it has bought back 511,400 shares.

data.title: Montenegro eyes 200 MW of new PV capacity in next three years
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), March 21 (SeeNews) - Montenegrin state-run power utility Elektroprivreda Crne Gore (EPCG) plans to connect to the grid some 200 MW of new solar energy capacities in the coming three years, its director general Ivan Bulatovic said.

The country has already connected 70 MW of small rooftop solar systems, becoming a regional leader in the prosumer concept, and plans to raise this figure to 100 MW by the end of the year, Bulatovic said in a press release on Thursday.

EPCG has been promoting renewable energy production through its series of Solari projects, targeting the installation of 100 MW of rooftop photovoltaic (PV) panels. The first phase of the project - comprising Solari 3000+ for households and Solari 500+ for businesses - was launched at the end of 2021 and completed at the end of 2023. It included the installation of 30 MW of green energy capacities. Solari 5000+, launched in 2024 with an estimated budget of 70 million euro ($75.8 million), targets the installation of a further 70 MW capacity.

Bulatovic also pointed out to the significance of the long-planned 82 MW Krusevo hydropower project, which has been developed in cooperation with French state-owned energy firm Electricite de France (EDF).

EPCG has started preparing the technical documentation for the construction of several photovoltaic plants, including the 47 MWp Krupac project, the 137 MW Stedim project, and the Kapino Polje system of four solar farms of combined 68 MW, among others.

The power utility's installed generation capacity totals 877 MW, with 649 MW coming from its two big hydro power plants, Perucica and Piva, and 225 MW contributed by the country's sole thermal power plant - TE Pljevlja. EPCG also operates several small hydropower plants.

($ = 0.923 euro)

data.title: Pure Health Board Resolves on Early Settlement of Banking Facilities
data.description:
Pure Health Holding's (ADX:PUREHEALTH) board approved the early settlement of 1.85 billion-Emirati-dirham banking facilities.

The approval for the settlement of the facilities obtained from First Abu Dhabi Bank was granted at the healthcare company's board meeting on Friday, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Continental Holdings Unit Sells 21 Land Lots in Taiwan
data.description:
Continental Holdings unit Continental Development sold 21 land lots in New Taipei City, Taiwan, for NT$553.7 million.

The land, spanning about 22,323 square meters, was sold to an unrelated individual buyer, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The estimated gain from the disposal is NT$356 million, the project developer added.

data.title: Viva Energy Group Says Over 25 Million Ordinary Shares to be Released from Escrow
data.description:
Viva Energy Group said 25.3 million ordinary shares issued on March 28, 2024, to SEPL, will be released from voluntary escrow on March 28, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The shares were part consideration for the acquisition of the OTR Group and were subject to a voluntary escrow for a period of 12 months from completion.

A further 25.3 ordinary shares issued to SEPL in connection with the acquisition remain voluntarily escrowed for a further 12 months until March 28, 2026.

SEPL is an entity associated with Yasser Shahin, and Shahin informed the firm that he has no present intention of divesting the shares coming out of escrow. Shahin now holds around 62 million shares, representing 3.9% of the firm's issued capital.

data.title: Romania's Jan industrial sales edge down on manufacturing downtick
data.description:
BUCHAREST (Romania), March 21 (SeeNews) - Romania’s industrial sales inched down by 0.4% year-on-year in January, driven by a 1% annual downtick in manufacturing sector sales, the statistical office, INS, said on Thursday.

Conversely, sales in the mining sector rose by an annual 16.3% in January, INS said in a data release.

Sales of capital goods decreased by 6.5% on the year in January, while sales of durable and non-durable goods rose by 15.2% and 5%, respectively. Sales of intermediary goods increased by an annual 3.8%, while energy sales fell 9.5% on the year.

In 2024, Romania’s industrial sales climbed by 5.6%.

Romania’s industrial sales, breakdown by sector (percentage change):

Jan y/yJan m/m
Total-0.4-7.1
-mining+16.3-7.6
-manufacturing-1.0-7.1

Source: INS

data.title: Nikkei Slips On Tariff Fears As BOJ Holds Rates Steady
data.description:
Japanese shares ended lower on Friday on tariff fears and geopolitical tensions as the Bank of Japan held rates steady.

The Nikkei 225 fell 0.2%, or 74.82 points, to end at 37,677.06.

The Bank of Japan held rates steady on Wednesday despite signs of further tightening from wages and inflation. Markets expect a 25 bps hike by year-end, but Governor Kazuo Ueda warned that US tariff fallout could affect timing.

Inflation eased in February, with headline CPI slowing to 3.7% from January's two-year high of 4%, helped by resumed electricity and gas subsidies.

Core CPI rose 3% year-on-year, down from 3.2% in January. Electricity and gas price increases slowed to 9% and 3.4%, while food prices, excluding perishables, climbed 5.6%, up from 5.1%. Rent edged up 0.2%.

Odakyu Electric Railway reported a 1.8% rise in February passenger numbers, with revenue up 1.3% to nearly 3.3 billion yen. Department store sales fell 0.6%, while Shinjuku hotel occupancy rose 4.3 points to 91.7%.

Japan Airlines cut its full-year revenue forecast to 1.84 trillion yen, down 4.7%, but left profit and dividend targets unchanged.

Next Generation Technology Group revised its 2024 results, raising total assets to 15.4 billion yen from 14.7 billion yen and net assets to 4 billion yen from 3.11 billion yen.

data.title: Malaysia Airlines parent to buy 30 Boeing 737 MAX jets
data.description:
Key points:
  • MAG to buy 18 Boeing 737 MAX 8, 12 MAX 10 jets
  • Malaysia Airlines modernising aging fleet after safety concerns
  • Group to consider option for 30 more jets depending on market conditions

Malaysia Aviation Group, the parent company of Malaysia Airlines, said on Friday it will purchase 30 new Boeing 737 aircraft for 2029 delivery, as it seeks to modernise the flag-carrier's fleet and rebound from a series of setbacks.

The group has agreed to buy 18 Boeing 737 MAX 8 and 12 of the 737 MAX 10 planes, MAG managing director Izham Ismail said. MAG declined to reveal the value of the deal.

The group includes Malaysia Airlines, short-haul airline Firefly and pilgrimage airline Amal, with a mixed fleet that has both Airbus and Boeing planes. It was not immediately clear how many of the new jets would be allocated to each carrier.

Malaysia Airlines has struggled to recover from two deadly disasters in 2014 and has been steadily renewing its fleet. The airline was delisted in 2014 and its former parent Malaysian Airline System was restructured into MAG under the ownership of sovereign wealth fund Khazanah Nasional [RIC:RIC:KHAZA.UL].

Malaysia Airlines temporarily reduced its flights after it was investigated last year by Malaysia's civil aviation authority, which found several significant safety and maintenance issues.

"It's very timely that Malaysia Airlines does not repeat the same mistake. So, the ordering of new planes must be well-timed, otherwise we will end up as we did last year," Izham told a press conference, describing the purchase as a very important milestone.

The decision to buy the aircraft was based on extensive evaluation such as fuel efficiency, environmental sustainability, operational performance, and technical reliability, Izham said.

The deal included an option to purchase a further 30 jets, which he said would depend on market conditions.

"Malaysia Airlines has to prove its credibility, that it is able to sustain its trajectory sustainably, commercially, and financially, then we'll make that decision," he said.

In February 2022, MAG agreed to lease 25 new Boeing 737 MAX 8 jets from Air Lease Corporation for delivery by 2026. The first of the planes arrived in November 2023. MAG in 2022 also said it would acquire 20 new Airbus A330neo jets by 2028, the first of which arrived in November.

Izham said they have already received 11 aircraft from the 2022 agreement on the Boeing jets and the remaining 14 would arrive in the next 18 months.

Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim, who attended the signing, said he was pleased to see Malaysia Airlines continue its longstanding relationship with Boeing.

"I trust that Boeing and GE Aerospace will recognise this partnership as a valuable opportunity to strengthen their presence in Malaysia,” he said.

data.title: Arch Meter Seeks Three-Month Extension for Debt Issue
data.description:
Arch Meter will apply for a three-month extension to complete the fundraising of its first domestic unsecured convertible corporate bonds worth NT$400 million.

The electronic measuring meter manufacturer cited market conditions and the need to maximize shareholder value as key reasons for the extension, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Arch Meter will submit a formal application, and will provide further updates upon securing regulatory approval, it added.

data.title: Orora Buys Back 431,724 Shares
data.description:
Orora repurchased 431,724 fully-paid ordinary shares in an on-market buyback for AU$862,196, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

UBS and Jarden acted as the broker for the transaction.

It plans to buy back a maximum of 134.3 million shares under the program.

data.title: UK red tape, red ink, and Rachel's rules
data.description:
UK RED TAPE, RED INK, AND RACHEL'S RULES

Britain's government gets another report card on its efforts to curtail spending while Germany, in contrast, goes for a final vote on its big borrow-to-spend splash on Friday.

The UK government finances report should show how deeply in the red the government is as the financial year draws to a close and just days ahead of finance minister Rachel Reeves's budget update on March 26.

Government departments have been tightening their purse strings to help Reeves meet her budget reduction goals, which have been trammelled by rising gilt yields and a slowing economy.

Last week, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer pledged to "hack back the thicket of red tape" suffocating the economy and the British government abolished NHS England, bringing the health service back under direct ministerial control.

Reeves has set herself an ambitious target of balancing day-to-day spending and tax revenues by the 2029-30 financial year. She is expected to announce she has rebuilt a 9.9 billion pound ($12.83 billion) fiscal buffer, Bloomberg reported on Wednesday citing an unnamed source.

In Germany, on the other hand, investors have been celebrating the almost certain passage on Friday of legislation to create a 500 billion euro ($542 billion) fund for infrastructure and higher spending on defence.

The plan was approved this week in the lower house Bundestag, giving conservative leader and chancellor-in-waiting Friedrich Merz a huge boost and investors reason to hope for a recovery in Europe's largest economy.

The legislation goes today to the Bundesrat upper house but appears certain to pass there.

Meanwhile in broader markets, Wall Street's bullish momentum earlier this week, inspired by Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's view that the economy is in good shape and tariff-related price rises will be transitory, seems to have fizzled out.

Treasuries and the dollar are up, indicating a broader "risk-off" tone at play, as is gold, which has already rocketed 16% this year to record highs.

Policymakers across the globe have struck a cautious note in a week filled with central bank meetings as uncertainty in global economics and politics grew. The U.S. Federal Reserve, the Bank Of Japan and the Bank of England all held rates steady.

Key developments that could influence markets on Friday:

DATA: UK public finances (February), Euro zone current account (January), Euro zone consumer confidence (March, flash estimate), Canada retail sales (February)

SPEAKERS: Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee, New York Fed President John Williams, European Central Bank's Jose Luis Escriva at an event in Barcelona.

($1 = 0.9221 euros)

($1 = 0.7719 pounds)

(Vidya Ranganathan)

*****

** Stock rises as the infrastructure co receives a work order worth 168.9 million rupees (~$2 million) from the Ministry Of Defense

** More than 5.8 mln shares change hands, 2.6x its 30-day avg

** RAIT eyes fourth consecutive session of gains

** Stock up ~6% so far this month

data.title: Naura Technology Buys More Stake in Kingsemi for 1.45 Billion Yuan; Naura's Shares Drop 3%
data.description:
Naura Technology Group agreed to acquire an additional 8.4% stake in semiconductor maker Kingsemi for 1.45 billion yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Friday.

The stake will be bought from Shenyang Zhongke Tiansheng Automation Technology.

The additional stake will bring up the Chinese electronic components manufacturer's holdings in Kingsemi to 17.9%, the disclosure said.

Naura's shares slipped less than 3%, Kingsemi's shares rose over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: European Markets Head for Lower Open
data.description:
European equity markets were set for a weaker open on Friday, extending losses from the previous session as global economic and trade uncertainties continued to weigh on sentiment.

Earlier this week, the US Federal Reserve lowered its economic growth forecast and raised its inflation outlook, fueling concerns about potential stagflation.

Meanwhile, the Bank of England and Sweden’s Riksbank left their policies unchanged, while the Swiss National Bank unexpectedly cut its benchmark rate.

European Central Bank policymakers also signaled room for rate cuts this year, citing mounting risks from global trade tensions.

Investors will now focus on Eurozone current account data for January and consumer confidence figures for March.

In premarket trade, futures for the Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 were down about 0.5%.

data.title: Rio Tinto Has 'Strong Desire' to Invest More in U.S., Copper Chief Says
data.description:
By Rhiannon Hoyle

Rio Tinto, the world's second-biggest miner by market value, wants to increase its investment in the U.S., especially in copper, the head of the company's copper business said, after President Trump signed an executive order to streamline permitting processes and boost government financing for minerals projects.

"We have a strong desire to invest more in the U.S., particularly in copper," Katie Jackson said in emailed remarks Friday.

"It's clear that under the Trump administration, there is an increasing recognition of the need for domestic sources of copper and other critical materials in the U.S., to support manufacturing and the country's energy future," Jackson said.

Rio Tinto is the majority owner and operator of the Resolution copper project, located in Arizona, which it has estimated could supply as much as a quarter of the country's current demand for the industrial metal. It started the permitting process for the project, which has faced repeated legal challenges, in 2013.

Rio Tinto also runs the Kennecott copper operation in Utah, where it has been expanding mining underground and, in 2023, completed a major rebuild of its smelter. The company produces several other materials in the U.S., including lithium, borates and tellurium.

President Trump's executive order includes measures designed to boost U.S. minerals production and reduce America's reliance on supplies from other countries. China is the world's dominant supplier of many critical materials.

"Our national and economic security are now acutely threatened by our reliance upon hostile foreign powers' mineral production," the order said.

Write to Rhiannon Hoyle at rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com

data.title: Indian gold loan provider Manappuram hits record high as Bain Capital plans stake buy
data.description:
Shares of India's Manappuram Finance jumped 6% on Friday after analysts said that Bain Capital's plans to buy an 18% stake and jointly control the gold loan provider will help ease its management succession plans.

U.S. private equity firm Bain Capital will subscribe to Manappuram's shares and warrants at 236 rupees apiece, at a 9% premium over the stock's closing price on Thursday of 217.5 rupees.

After the investment, which is valued at $508 million, Bain Capital will jointly control Manappuram with other large shareholders, termed as 'promoters' under Indian regulations.

Manappuram's shares rose as much as 6.3% to 231.08 rupees - their highest level on record - as of 12:05 p.m. IST.

The company's founder and CEO V.P. Nandakumar, who has been at the helm for almost four decades, will become the company's non-executive chairman post the investment, it said on Thursday, with investors now eyeing nominations for the top role.

Bain Capital will also have the capacity to influence strategic decisions at Manappuram, including the right to nominate for the appointment of key roles, such as the CEO, which analysts at Jefferies and CLSA see as a key positive from the deal.

"We see the possibility of healthy re-rating in Manappuram stock as new management takes over," CLSA analysts said in a note.

Jefferies and CLSA raised their price targets on the stock after the announcement by 14.6% and 20%, respectively. They have "buy" and "outperform" ratings on the stock.

The deal, which comes at a time when record prices have made gold mortgaging appealing, is expected to close in the next financial year and will aid growth in Manappuram's gold loan business.

Gold loans contribute 75% of the company's overall revenue.

Jefferies also said that a part of the capital raised from the stake sale may be utilized to absorb losses in Manappuram's microfinance business, which was hit by uncertainty in market conditions and regulatory scrutiny .

Its unit, Asirvad Micro Finance would withdraw the IPO draft papers, Manappuram said on Thursday.

data.title: Compucase Enterprise Acquires Amber Investment Partners for $43 Million
data.description:
Compucase Enterprise's board approved the acquisition of Amber Investment Partners for $42.9 million.

Under the deal, the PC cases manufacturer will buy 1.9 million shares from Amber VC Investment and Vistra Technology, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

The deal, subject to regulatory approval, will allow for more flexible expansion of its production bases and help secure additional orders, it added.
